_comment: sluice hardened seccomp profile (opt-in via SLUICE_SECCOMP=hardened). Allow-by-default minus a dangerous syscall class the sandbox never needs: in-container namespace creation (the userns/CVE surface), tracing, key material, mount, kernel-module/kexec/swap. Sessions already run uid 1000 with zero caps, so this is defence in depth. NOTE: blocking unshare/clone(NEWUSER) breaks browser-engine sandboxes (Chromium/Playwright) - run those with the engine's default profile or the browser's --no-sandbox. Targets amd64/arm64 (clone flags = arg0).

syscalls._comment: clone3 -> ENOSYS so glibc falls back to clone(), which we then filter for CLONE_NEWUSER. Preserves normal thread/fork creation.
block clone() only when CLONE_NEWUSER (0x10000000) is set; normal threads/forks (no NEWUSER) are allowed.
